metadata.scheme_name: Supply of School Uniform , Shoes Socks to all Students for Std IX to XII
metadata.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

raw_data.details: Provision of complete uniform(Shirt, Pants, Skirt, Tie, Belt) socks, shoes to all students in classes IX to XII of Government schools. It ensures that every child, regardless of their socio-economic background, has the basic physical tools required for a productive learning environment. Standardized uniform scheme to promote discipline and equality among male and female students, removing social distinctions based on attire.
raw_data.benefits: 1. Fosters a sense of identity, equality and discipline among students. 2. Significantly reduces school absenteeism and improves performance by eliminating the financial barrier of purchasing clothes. 3. Providing shoes and socks promotes better foot health and hygiene for students commuting to school. 4. Promotes regular school attendance by supporting foundational needs
raw_data.eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a student. 2. The applicant should be studying in class 9th to 12th. 3. The applicant should be studying in a government school in UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineNo separate application is required. Eligible students enrolled in government schools are automatically covered under the scheme.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityDadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu Supply of School Uniform , Shoes Socks to all Students for Std IX to XIIEducationFree Shoes SocksFree StationeryDetailsProvision of complete uniform(Shirt, Pants, Skirt, Tie, Belt) socks, shoes to all students in classes IX to XII of Government schools. It ensures that every child, regardless of their socio-economic background, has the basic physical tools required for a productive learning environment. Standardized uniform scheme to promote discipline and equality among male and female students, removing social distinctions based on attire. Benefits1. Fosters a sense of identity, equality and discipline among students. 2. Significantly reduces school absenteeism and improves performance by eliminating the financial barrier of purchasing clothes. 3. Providing shoes and socks promotes better foot health and hygiene for students commuting to school. 4. Promotes regular school attendance by supporting foundational needs Eligibility1. The applicant should be a student. 2. The applicant should be studying in class 9th to 12th. 3. The applicant should be studying in a government school in UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu. Application ProcessOfflineNo separate application is required. Eligible students enrolled in government schools are automatically covered under the scheme. Documents RequiredNo Documents requiredFrequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the purpose of this scheme? To encourage regular attendance and continuity in education among school students. Which students are eligible? Students from Classes 9 to 12 studying in government schools. Are private school students eligible? No, the scheme is only for government school students. How many uniforms are provided? Two pairs of uniforms per student. Is there any application fee? No, the application is completely free. Is the scheme for both boys and girls? Yes, it benefits both boys and girls equally. Sources And References[Supply Of School Unifrom, Shoes Socks To All Students For IX To XII](https://ddd.gov.in/directorate-of-education/)OkWas this helpful?

raw_data.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

faqs.question: What is the purpose of this scheme?
faqs.answer: To encourage regular attendance and continuity in education among school students.

faqs.question: Which students are eligible?
faqs.answer: Students from Classes 9 to 12 studying in government schools.

faqs.question: Are private school students eligible?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only for government school students.

faqs.question: How many uniforms are provided?
faqs.answer: Two pairs of uniforms per student.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, the application is completely free.

faqs.question: Is the scheme for both boys and girls?
faqs.answer: Yes, it benefits both boys and girls equally.
